notes: Native programme subissue and Project 28 record created.
Added numerical and resource budgets, deterministic parallelism, registry-derived claim conformance, ADR and deprecation validation, adversarial fixtures, and controlled ecosystem-drift proposals.
Default branch has five open JupyterLab alerts: two high, two medium, and one low. All report 4.6.2 as the first patched version; remediation and closure are required before release.

command: gh pr list --repo edithatogo/voiage --state all --search author:app/renovate
notes: The repository has no Renovate-authored PR or dependency dashboard evidence. Renovate becomes the sole version-update configuration in source, but Dependabot security updates remain enabled until App activation and one checked update PR are verified.

Renovate configuration validated with managers for Cargo, GitHub Actions, git submodules, npm and PEP 621; vulnerability updates skip the ordinary queue, while security, major, numerical, lockfile and submodule updates cannot automerge.
Raised the JupyterLab floor and lock from 4.6.1 to 4.6.2, GitHub's first patched version for all five open alerts. Alert closure remains default-branch evidence after merge.
Preserved the active signed-commit, squash-only, linear-history, strict-current-head, thread-resolution and required-check controls and added Reproducible build and SBOM as a required status check.

Secret scanning and push protection remain enabled with no open secret alerts. GitHub retained non-provider patterns and validity checks as disabled after two enable attempts, so those controls remain an entitlement-dependent external gate.

Renovate covers every detected dependency surface, including PEP 621, Cargo, npm, Pixi, pre-commit, Docker Compose, GitHub Actions and git submodules. All listed local environments passed; Python 3.12-3.14 and dependency frontier suites passed, and total coverage was 91.01%.
